generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables in AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helper declarations to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
Range guarantees often follow the pattern: `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing unqualified package-qualified identifiers or data types in AGREE contracts.
Do not attempt to fix AGREE errors by modifying the AADL architecture.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, owners)
